channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DMK sources suggest AIADMK should end alliance with BJP in Tamil Nadu
item.description: According to sources from the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), the AIADMK party in Tamil Nadu should reconsider its alliance with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). The DMK sources argue that forming a...

item.title: Australia’s Approach to Reintegrating Returning ISIS-Linked Families
item.description: A group of four women and nine children with links to Islamic State have returned to Australia from Syria, drawing significant media attention. Three of the women were arrested upon arrival in Melbour...

item.title: Vanessa Mdee releases heartfelt rendition of classic hymn ‘How Great Thou Art’
item.description: Vanessa Mdee, the well-known Tanzanian Afropop and Bongo Flava artiste, has released a moving version of the classic Christian hymn 'How Great Thou Art'. This song, originally from the Swedish poem “O...

item.title: Kingston Council Explores Options to Manage Future Budget Pressures
item.description: Kingston, Ontario, is taking proactive steps to address potential long-term budget challenges. During a council meeting on Tuesday, city council unanimously directed staff to investigate various strat...

item.title: Coalition pushes anti-political dynasty bill limiting family members in public office
item.description: A coalition called 'Dapat Isa Lang,' led by former Commission on Elections Commissioner Luie Guia, is launching a signature campaign to push for an Anti-Political Dynasty law in the Philippines. The p...

item.title: Labour Party facing significant electoral losses in upcoming vote
item.description: The Labour Party appears to be heading towards a substantial defeat in the forthcoming elections, with recent polling data indicating historically low support levels. Analysts suggest that a combinati...

item.title: Wayne Bennett criticises NRL for early release of Latrell Mitchell's withdrawal
item.description: Wayne Bennett, the coach of the South Sydney Rabbitohs, has publicly criticised the National Rugby League (NRL) after Latrell Mitchell's withdrawal from the upcoming match against Cronulla was reveale...

item.title: Google Launches Screenless Fitbit Air and Updates Fitbit App to Google Health
item.description: Google has introduced a new wearable device, the Fitbit Air, which follows the trend of screenless health trackers. Unlike traditional smartwatches, the Fitbit Air does not have a display; instead, it...

item.title: Farmers in Koruvada urge authorities to correct land records after resurvey
item.description: Farmers from Koruvada village in K. Kotapadu mandal, Anakapalli district, have raised concerns about discrepancies in their land records after the recent Jagananna resurvey programme. Eight farmers cu...

item.title: Government to Transform Kiganjo Police College into a University for Police Training
item.description: Interior CS Kipchumba Murkomen has revealed plans by the national government to upgrade Kiganjo Police Training College into a fully-fledged university. Speaking during a visit with Inspector General ...

item.title: Kayole boy and family receive government-supported 3-bedroom house
item.description: Tito Wambua, a young boy from Kayole, Nairobi, who was recently seen caring for his younger siblings, has received a major boost in life. President William Ruto personally gifted the family a three-be...

item.title: BMW Reports 25% Drop in Q1 Profit Amid Tariff Pressures and Global Competition
item.description: German carmaker BMW has reported a sharp 25% decline in its first-quarter pretax earnings for 2026, amounting to €2.3 billion (around R44.57 billion). This was slightly above analysts’ expectations of...

item.title: Yanga SC Dismisses Coach Gonçalves Despite Unbeaten League Run
item.description: Young Africans SC, popularly known as Yanga, has surprisingly parted ways with Head Coach Pedro Gonçalves even though the team was leading the Mainland Premier League without losing any match. The clu...

item.title: Analysis of English Council Election Results with Maps and Charts
item.description: The article aimed to provide an analysis of the ongoing English council elections, summarising trends and insights from the results reported so far. Through the use of maps and charts, it was intended...

item.title: Cruise Industry Remains Popular Despite Health Risks, Experts Say
item.description: The cruise industry continues to attract millions of passengers globally, despite recurring health incidents such as COVID-19, norovirus, and recent hantavirus outbreaks. Experts explain that outbreak...

item.title: Tinubu Aide Claims Many Online Peter Obi Supporters Are Bots, Few Are Real
item.description: Daniel Bwala, the Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication, don yan say plenty supporters of former Presidential candidate Peter Obi, wey dem dey call ‘Obidients’, no really be...

item.title: WHO confirms low public risk from hantavirus after cruise ship cases
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has reassured the public that the risk of hantavirus spreading widely is very low, following the recent outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. The ship, carryi...

item.title: Three women linked to IS face charges in Australia over alleged crimes in Syria
item.description: Three women associated with IS-linked families have been charged with serious offences after returning to Australia from Middle Eastern camps. Two of the women, aged 53 and 31, appeared separately in ...

item.title: Bwala Criticizes Opposition’s Disunity Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Public Communications and Media, Daniel Bwala, don yarn say plenty politicians wey dey opposition na 'internally displaced' and dem dey desperate for po...

item.title: Tanzania Water Ministry to Fund Projects Using Bonds and Boost Private Sector Participation
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through the Ministry of Water, has received approval from the National Assembly for a budget of 1.12 trillion shillings for the 2026/27 financial year. Minister Jumaa Aweso c...
